content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master`, inspected the process backend changes and related tests, and found 1 correctness issue: the new `-fast` suffix parsing leaks into OpenRouter model handling.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: The new `-fast` suffix handling is applied to OpenRouter requests as well as Codex. `build_command` feeds OpenRouter model IDs through `codex_new_session_args` / `codex_resume_args` (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:954,979-991`), and those helpers now strip any trailing `-fast` and inject `service_tier="fast"` (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1319-1404`). That means an OpenRouter model whose real ID ends in `-fast` will be silently rewritten to a different model and may 404 or target the wrong backend model. The added tests only exercise Codex paths (`tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs:943-1198`), so this regression is currently unguarded. severity: medium. affected_files: [src/adapters/process_backend.rs, tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs]. remediation_hint: Gate `-fast` parsing and `service_tier` injection to `BackendFamily::Codex` only, and add a regression test proving OpenRouter `...-fast` model IDs are passed through unchanged.
   Rationale: The bead is specifically about Codex fast-mode suffixes, but the implementation changed a shared helper that is also used by the supported OpenRouter path. This is a direct regression introduced by the current change and should be corrected before landing.
